public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as communal duty supervisor.
manner: Reads as a functional local operator shaped by place, duty, and the practical customs around them. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a communal duty supervisor still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of communal duty supervisor in and around Embervale Settlement, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Embervale Settlement workable through the obligations of communal duty supervisor while managing the immediate pressure of overcrowding in Soreheim Alliance.
hidden_pressure: Must stay useful at the seam where policy becomes ordinary consequence. Around Embervale Settlement, that usually means carrying overcrowding, sickness, heat-failure risk without letting the role of communal duty supervisor visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Getting involved with Kairn Steelbrood can expose the party to the everyday consequences of pressure that others only discuss in the abstract.
Kairn Steelbrood can offer grounded local knowledge once the party proves it is useful rather than merely curious.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Kairn Steelbrood carries the look of a practiced local operator whose authority is read through place, familiarity, and the reactions of others more than formal insignia. In Embervale Settlement, this lands inside a public field already shaped by regional quartermasters, shift-boss families.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Embervale Settlement: the part of Embervale Settlement where their work actually matters day to day
who_avoids_them: those who only appear when things break
